summary: Iteration 21 â€” Confirmation pass for the native Windows Bridge installer experience. Backend: 3/3 pytest tests PASSED in 2.4s (public GET /api/bridge/installer/windows returns {available:false, version:'1.0.0', url:null, message:'L\'installer Ă¨ in fase di pubblicazione.'} with NO auth needed; full native-agent handshake: POST /api/bridge/agents (admin) â†’ 6-digit pairing_code â†’ POST /api/bridge/pair returns long-lived token â†’ POST /api/bridge/relay/heartbeat 200 with {ok:true, server_time}; GET /api/bridge/relay/poll 200 with {jobs:[], count:0}; POST /api/bridge/logs with consent:true returns {ok:true, stored:true}, with consent:false returns {ok:false, stored:false}; unauthenticated /api/bridge/logs correctly rejected with 401; re-pairing an already-consumed code returns 404). Frontend Playwright: full mobile-first Bridge setup UI verified â€” /app/setup/bridge loads, bridge-toggle-advanced is COLLAPSED by default (weekly-summary/upload-sample not in DOM), 'Connect the Bridge' section visible with 'New Bridge' button. Clicking bridge-create-agent creates an unpaired agent card that renders ALL required testids: bridge-stepper-<id> (steps: 'Installation' / 'Connecting' / 'Connected'), bridge-download-<id> (button text 'Download Ordia Bridge'), bridge-pairing-code-<id> (e.g. '650657'), bridge-qr-<id> (QRCodeSVG), bridge-waiting-<id> (amber pulse banner). NO docker run/docker-compose/terminal commands anywhere on the page â€” the only 'docker' occurrence is the marketing phrase 'No commands, no Docker'. Clicking Download Ordia Bridge with installer unavailable shows a friendly EN info toast: 'The Ordia Bridge installer will be available shortly. Contact support to receive it.' (not an error/crash). Advanced toggle expandâ†’reveals bridge-upload-sample (ERP format builder), click againâ†’collapses (removed from DOM). i18n verified end-to-end: EN default renders 'Installation/Connecting/Connected' + 'Download Ordia Bridge'; clicking the đź‡®đź‡ą flag switches to IT which renders 'Installazione/Connessione/Collegato' + 'Scarica Ordia Bridge' + all other labels (Impostazioni avanzate, Collega il Bridge, Nuovo Bridge, In attesa del collegamentoâ€¦) â€” NO raw i18n keys leaked. Regression: bridge-agent-delete-<id> removes the agent card cleanly, and no console errors. Cleanup verified via direct Mongo: bridge_agents=[], bridge_logs count=0 â€” demo DB restored to pristine state.
critical_code_review_comments: bridge.py:236-247 installer_windows() is correctly PUBLIC (no auth Depends) and safely reads env â€” returns {available:false, url:null, version, message:'L\'installer Ă¨ in fase di pubblicazione.'} when BRIDGE_INSTALLER_URL unset. Optional ?redirect=1 does a 302 to the release asset once URL is populated â€” perfect for the native agent auto-update path.

bridge.py:341-350 upload_bridge_logs() correctly depends on get_current_agent (X-Bridge-Token) BEFORE consent check â†’ unauthenticated requests hit 401 in the dependency, never reaching the handler. Consent gate stores only when consent=True; logs truncated to 50000 chars. Consider adding created_at TTL index on bridge_logs (currently unbounded growth).

BridgeSetup.js:171-238 AgentCard has clean separation: unpaired branch renders the 3-step onboarding block (Download â†’ Enter code â†’ QR + waiting banner), paired branch just renders the stepper. downloadInstaller() correctly handles unavailable state (data.available===false) with toast.info fallback + generic toast.error for network failures â€” no crash.

BridgeSetup.js:391-395 advanced toggle uses conditional rendering (showAdvanced && <section>...) rather than CSS display:none, so advanced components (weekly summary, ERP format builder, adapters, delivery log) are literally absent from DOM when collapsed â€” perfect for the 'simple by default' requirement and lowers React tree cost.
AgentCard receives `simple={!showAdvanced}` so in the simple view the ERP name + export profile selectors are hidden. Good UX split.
I18nContext.js:258-279 all Bridge-simplified-setup strings (17 keys) have explicit EN translations, verified rendered live via language switch. No missing keys, no fallback-to-source visible.

One tiny inconsistency: BridgeSetup.js:349 toast for agent creation uses t('Agente creato â€” usa il codice per accoppiarlo') but that IT source has an EN mapping in I18nContext.js â€” this works. Just flagging for consistency: might be cleaner to use canonical EN 'Agent created â€” use the code to pair it' as the source key long-term.

server.py is now 3265+ lines â€” the split-out backlog (Bridge, Leads, Notifications) mentioned in iter17-20 is still pending. bridge.py is already modular (963 lines) and correctly wired via setup_bridge(api, ctx). Good pattern to replicate.

Native agent files (/app/bridge_native/*, /app/.github/workflows/build-bridge.yml) NOT runtime-tested here (Linux env can't execute .exe/Inno Setup). Only app-side validated. Windows build verification must happen in GitHub Actions.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter21_bridge_installer.py (NEW â€” 3 tests, ~150 lines, inline dotenv loader, module-scoped cleanup deletes created bridge_agents + bridge_logs via direct Mongo)

success_rate.backend: 100% (3/3 in test_iter21_bridge_installer.py, 2.4s: installer public unavailable-state + full pairâ†’heartbeatâ†’pollâ†’logs(consent T/F)â†’unauth reject + admin list/delete)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (bridge-toggle-advanced collapsed by default, bridge-create-agent â†’ all 5 required testids (stepper/download/pairing-code/qr/waiting) rendered, download click â†’ friendly EN info toast, advanced expand/collapse toggles DOM sections, ENâ†”IT stepper + download labels verified with 0 raw key leaks, delete agent removes card, no console errors)

seed_data_creation: 1 throwaway bridge agent created per backend test + 1 UI-created agent. ALL cleaned up: (a) backend module-scoped cleanup fixture deletes bridge_agents + bridge_logs by id via direct pymongo, (b) UI test explicitly clicked bridge-agent-delete-<id> at the end. Final DB state verified: db.bridge_agents.count_documents({})=0, db.bridge_logs.count_documents({})=0 â€” identical to pre-test state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter21 focus: Native Windows Bridge installer flow. Endpoints tested: GET /api/bridge/installer/windows (public, no auth), POST /api/bridge/agents (admin), POST /api/bridge/pair (public, consumes pairing_code, returns token), POST /api/bridge/relay/heartbeat + GET /api/bridge/relay/poll + POST /api/bridge/logs (all X-Bridge-Token auth). Frontend route: /app/setup/bridge. Key testids: bridge-toggle-advanced, bridge-create-agent, bridge-start-big (empty-state alt), bridge-stepper-<id>, bridge-download-<id>, bridge-pairing-code-<id>, bridge-qr-<id>, bridge-waiting-<id>, bridge-agent-delete-<id>. Advanced sections (bridge-weekly-summary, bridge-upload-sample, bridge-proposed-profile, bridge-profile-<id>) only in DOM when showAdvanced=true. Language switcher lives in the top-right header (flag buttons aria-label='Italiano' / 'English'). To re-run backend: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_iter21_bridge_installer.py -v. BRIDGE_INSTALLER_URL env is UNSET on purpose (installer built by GitHub Actions, published via webhook) â€” installer_windows returns available:false and the UI download button shows a friendly info toast; DO NOT set the env var in this run. Native agent code (/app/bridge_native/, /app/.github/workflows/build-bridge.yml) is NOT runtime-testable here (Linux env), only syntax-checkable â€” leave to GitHub Actions.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All P0 requirements met: (1) installer endpoint public + returns proper unavailable-state contract, (2) full native-agent handshake works, (3) consent gate on /bridge/logs stores/rejects correctly, (4) unauthenticated /bridge/logs rejected with 401, (5) mobile-first simple UI is default with correct testids + no Docker/terminal command visible, (6) friendly toast on unavailable installer, (7) advanced toggle collapsed by default and expand/collapse works, (8) full ENâ†”IT i18n coverage with 0 key leaks, (9) regression on list/delete agent works. Main agent can self-test to confirm.
